weather.tip: A 92% chance of rain under overcast skies almost guarantees a wet day — carry an umbrella, leave earlier than usual for your commute, and expect the usual low-lying stretches to water-log.

around.headline: Bengaluru school PT teacher booked for murder after allegedly beating a 12-year-old student to death
around.summary: A Class 6 student died at a residential school and police have booked the PT teacher for murder over allegations he beat the child; parents allege assault. If your child seems reluctant to talk about school or shows unexplained marks, ask directly rather than waiting.
around.source: The Hindu / The Times of India / NDTV

around.headline: Road rage flares up again on the Bengaluru-Mysuru corridor
around.summary: A family was attacked near Maddur despite the wife offering the attackers gold to stop (three held), and separately KSRTC staff were assaulted in Mandya on the same stretch (two held). If you're driving this corridor, don't stop or engage if another vehicle tries to pull you over — head for a toll plaza or police checkpoint instead.
around.source: Deccan Herald / The New Indian Express / NDTV

around.headline: Monkey fever (KFD) cases climb to 214 as a delayed monsoon keeps the season alive
around.summary: Karnataka has recorded 214 Kyasanur Forest Disease cases this season, with the delayed monsoon extending the risk window. If you're trekking or visiting forest fringes around Bengaluru (Western Ghats-adjacent districts), use tick-repellent and wear covered footwear.

around.headline: Green Line Metro hit by its third technical glitch in 20 days
around.summary: BMRCL confirmed another glitch disrupted Green Line services during this morning's peak hours, the third such incident in under three weeks. Build in extra buffer time if you rely on this line today.
around.source: The Times of India / The Hindu / The New Indian Express

around.headline: Vehicle-towing drive begins today across Bengaluru's five city corporations
around.summary: Greater Bengaluru Authority's five corporations start removing abandoned vehicles from roads and footpaths from today; residents can report abandoned vehicles via the ASTraM app. Move any long-parked vehicle off a public street now to avoid it being towed.
around.source: The News Minute / The Times of India

around.headline: Bengaluru's second airport confirmed for the city's south
around.summary: CM DK Shivakumar announced the location for Bengaluru's upcoming second airport will be south of the city. It's an early-stage announcement — no timeline for construction yet.

around.headline: Iran-US tensions escalate even as oil prices slip
around.summary: Iran says it struck US military targets in the Gulf and fired ballistic missiles at a US command centre in Jordan, and is preparing to bury its slain Supreme Leader, but oil prices actually slipped despite the standoff. Worth keeping an eye on fuel prices in case that reverses if the conflict widens.

events.name: Happy Streets, Whitefield
events.when: Recently announced, check local listings for the exact date
events.note: Car-free open-streets morning returns to Whitefield — good for walking, cycling, and street performances without traffic.

events.name: Bengaluru International Short Film Festival (BISFF) — pre-show gala
events.when: Pre-show event held this week, main festival dates to follow
events.note: Citywide short-film festival's pre-show gala took place ahead of the main event.

events.name: Three-day classical dance celebration
events.note: A three-day classical dance festival is on in the city — worth catching if you're into Bharatanatyam/Kuchipudi-style performances.

events.name: "Grief and loss through botanical art" exhibition
events.note: A botanical art exhibition exploring grief and loss is showing in the city — a quieter, reflective outing.

street_smart.title: Your cab driver won't move unless you pay extra cash. What now?
street_smart.based_on: Evergreen consumer-rights rotation topic (auto/cab overcharging & refusal) — no matching real incident in today's feeds.
street_smart.situation: It's pouring rain and you've booked an app-based cab home from work. The driver calls and says he won't come to your exact pickup point, or wants ₹150 extra 'because of the rain,' or asks you to cancel the app booking and just pay him directly instead. What do you do?

choices.text: Agree to pay the extra cash the driver is asking for so you don't miss the ride in the rain.
choices.feedback: It gets you moving, but it trains drivers that harassment works, and you're left with no record if there's a dispute or safety issue later in the ride.

choices.text: Cancel the app booking and travel with him for a flat cash fare he quotes, without booking a fresh ride.
choices.feedback: This is the actual trap: once you're off the app, there's no trip record, no GPS trail, no verified driver ID tied to your ride, and no support to escalate to if the fare balloons mid-ride or something goes wrong. Drivers soliciting off-app cash rides are bypassing the very safety net you paid for.

choices.text: Argue loudly with the driver on the phone and threaten to report him immediately, refusing to consider booking any other cab.
choices.feedback: You're in the right, but escalating a confrontation over the phone (or worse, in person) can turn a fare dispute into something uglier, especially after dark. De-escalate first, document and report after.

choices.text: Don't negotiate — end the call, cancel via the app citing 'driver asked for extra cash / refused pickup,' and book again. If it keeps happening, note the driver ID and file a complaint with a screenshot of the fare.
choices.feedback: Correct. Cancelling and reporting through the app keeps the fare, trip record, and driver ID intact so the platform can act on it, and a documented pattern of refusals is exactly what gets repeat-offender drivers suspended.

takeaways: Never let a driver talk you off the app and onto a cash-only ride — that's the moment you lose the trip record, GPS trail, and support line that make an app cab safer than hailing one off the street.
Cancel and report the specific reason ('fare renegotiation' / 'driver refused pickup') through the app rather than just silently cancelling — that's what builds a case against repeat offenders.
If a driver gets aggressive in person, don't argue it out — move to a public, well-lit spot and report afterward. The fare dispute matters less than your safety.

quiz.q: Which Namma Metro line suffered its third technical glitch in under three weeks, disrupting this morning's peak-hour services?
quiz.why: BMRCL has now confirmed three separate Green Line glitches in about 20 days, including one at Yelachenahalli station earlier this week.

quiz.q: Iran said it fired ballistic missiles at a US military command centre located in which country?
quiz.why: Iran also reported separately striking US targets in the Gulf, as tensions escalated around its slain Supreme Leader's funeral.

quiz.q: From what date do Bengaluru's five city corporations begin removing abandoned vehicles from public roads and footpaths?
quiz.why: The Greater Bengaluru Authority's drive starts today; residents can report abandoned vehicles via the ASTraM app.

quiz.q: Under which Article of the Indian Constitution can a High Court be directly approached via writ petition for violation of a fundamental right?
quiz.why: Article 32 gives the same writ power to the Supreme Court — Article 226 is actually wider, since High Courts can also issue writs for 'any other purpose' beyond fundamental rights.

quiz.q: Kyasanur Forest Disease, or 'monkey fever' — currently active in Karnataka this season — is transmitted to humans mainly through the bite of which parasite?
quiz.why: The virus circulates among forest ticks and monkeys; human cases spike when people enter forest-fringe areas during the tick-active monsoon months.
